issue: rotate_point(pt, degrees) returns the wrong point for every nonzero angle. A 90-degree turn of (1, 0) should land on (0, 1), but it comes back near (-0.45, 0.89); a 180-degree turn of (3, 4) should give (-3, -4) but does not. The distance from the origin is preserved, so the point is being rotated -- just by the wrong amount. main.py delegates the actual rotation to a helper it imports from another module and forwards the angle straight through.
